title_en: 30. Exerting in sniffing in the water as long as you are not fasting is reported in the
content_en: hadith of Laqeet (radhiAllaahu 'anhu) who said: O Rasoolullah (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam), inform me of the wudoo . He said Complete the wudoo and rub between the fingers and exert in breathing in the water into the nose unless you are fasting. [Abu Dawood, no.l42; At-Tirmidhee, no.38; An-nasaa'ee, no.ll4; IbMaajah, no.407; and others]. (Declared as sahih by Ibn Hibbaan and al-Haalcim, and adh-Dhahabee agreed to that. Also declared as sahih by Ibn al Qaataan, AnNawawi and Ibn Hajr. Refer to the footnote in al-Baghawi's Sharh us-Sunnah: 1/417]. It is clear from these two ahaadith that washing the mouth and nose are both obligatory (waajib). Ibn Qudaamah says in 'al-Mughnee': Washing the nose and washing the mouth are both obligatory in both forms of purification - Ghusl and wudoo - as washing the face is obligatory in both of them in the established view of the madhab; and it is the saying of Ibn al-Mubaarak, Ibn Abi Lailaa and Ishaaq, and it is reported from 'Ataa. TAKING WATER INTO THE NOSE WITH THE RIGHT HAND AND EXPELLING IT WITH THE LEFT 'Abd Khair said: We were sitting looking towards 'Alee - as he made wudoo - and he entered into his mouth a handful of water with his right hand and washed his mouth and nose, then expelled it from his nose with his left hand - he did that three times, then said: Whoever would like to see the way of purification of Rasoolullah (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) - then this is his purification. [Reported by adDaarimee. Ash-shaikh al-Albaanee says in his notes on al-Mishkaat: Its isnaad is sahih]. WASHING THE FACE info@calgaryislam.com www.calgaryislam.com The face (wajh) is determined as being everything between the beginnings of the hair down to the cheeks and the chin, and up to the start of the ears including that which is between the beard and ear. Allaah ta'ala says: O Ye who believe! When ye prepare for prayer, wash your faces, and your hands (and arms) to the elbows; rub your heads (with water); and (wash) your feet to the ankles. [soorat-ul-Maa'idah,5:7] Humraan ibn Abaan narrates that 'Uthmaan (radhiAllaahu 'anhu) called for water to make wudoo and so mentioned the way in which the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) made wudoo . Humraan said: Then he washed his face three times. [AlBukhaaree (al-Fath, 1/312), Muslim (no.226)]. RUNNING WATER THROUGH THE BEARD WITH THE FINGERS 31. 'Uthmaan (radhiAllaahu'anhu) narrates that the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) used to run his (wet fingers) through his beard. [At-Tirmidhee (no.31), Ibn Maajah (no.430), Ibn Jaarood (p.43) and al-Haakim (1/14~) who declared its isnaad as sahih as did Ibn Khuzaimah and Ibn Hibbaan. See the note in al-Baghagawi's Sharh us-Sunnah, 1/421].
title_id: Hadis 30: Istinsyaq, Membasuh Wajah, dan Meratakan Air pada Jenggot
content_id: Tentang mengerahkan upaya dalam memasukkan air ke hidung selama tidak berpuasa, hal ini terdapat dalam hadis Laqit (radhiyallahu anhu) yang berkata: Wahai Rasulullah ﷺ, beritahukanlah kepadaku tentang wudu. Beliau bersabda: Sempurnakanlah wudu, ratakanlah air di antara jari-jari, dan bersungguhlah dalam memasukkan air ke hidung kecuali jika kamu sedang berpuasa. [Abu Dawud, no. 142; At-Tirmizi, no. 38; An-Nasai, no. 114; Ibnu Majah, no. 407; dan lainnya. Dinyatakan sahih oleh Ibnu Hibban dan Al-Hakim, dan Adz-Dzahabi menyetujuinya. Dinyatakan sahih pula oleh Ibnu al-Qattan, An-Nawawi, dan Ibnu Hajar]. Dari dua hadis ini jelas bahwa membasuh mulut dan hidung keduanya adalah wajib. Ibnu Qudamah berkata dalam Al-Mugni: Membasuh hidung dan membasuh mulut keduanya wajib dalam dua bentuk bersuci, yaitu mandi wajib dan wudu, sebagaimana membasuh wajah wajib dalam keduanya menurut pandangan yang tetap dalam mazhab. MENGAMBIL AIR KE HIDUNG DENGAN TANGAN KANAN DAN MENGELUARKANNYA DENGAN TANGAN KIRI. Abd Khair berkata: Kami sedang duduk memperhatikan Ali saat beliau berwudu. Beliau memasukkan ke dalam mulutnya satu telapak air dengan tangan kanan, berkumur dan beristinsyaq, kemudian mengeluarkannya dari hidungnya dengan tangan kiri. Beliau melakukan itu tiga kali, kemudian berkata: Siapa yang ingin melihat cara bersuci Rasulullah ﷺ, inilah cara bersuci beliau. [Diriwayatkan oleh Ad-Darimi. Syaikh Al-Albani berkata dalam catatannya atas Al-Misykat: Sanadnya sahih]. MEMBASUH WAJAH. Wajah ditentukan sebagai segala sesuatu yang ada di antara awal tumbuhnya rambut hingga pipi dan dagu, dan hingga awal telinga termasuk bagian antara jenggot dan telinga. Allah Taala berfirman: Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, apabila kamu hendak mendirikan salat, maka basuhlah wajahmu dan tanganmu sampai ke siku; usaplah kepalamu; dan basuhlah kakimu sampai ke kedua mata kaki. [Surah Al-Maidah 5:6]. Humran ibn Aban meriwayatkan bahwa Utsman (radhiyallahu anhu) meminta air untuk berwudu lalu menyebutkan cara Nabi ﷺ berwudu. Humran berkata: Kemudian beliau membasuh wajahnya tiga kali. [Al-Bukhari (al-Fath, 1/312); Muslim (no. 226)]. MEMASUKKAN AIR MELALUI JENGGOT DENGAN JARI. Utsman (radhiyallahu anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa meratakan air melalui jenggotnya dengan jari-jari basah. [At-Tirmizi (no. 31), Ibnu Majah (no. 430), Ibnu Jarud (hal. 43), dan Al-Hakim (1/149) yang menyatakan sanadnya sahih, demikian juga Ibnu Khuzaimah dan Ibnu Hibban].
